title: agastache rugosa var. hypoleuca
intro: Agastache rugosa var. hypoleuca, also known as Korean mint, is a perennial plant native to East Asia, characterized by its purple flowers and mint-like foliage. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, offering benefits like improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function. Agastache rugosa var. hypoleuca can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for various herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
